text: RESTOCK: Urfa biber (Turkish) back in stock — new harvest, smoky raisin notes. 250g and 1kg. Also fresh Tellicherry peppercorn and grade-A saffron threads. Reply ORDER to reserve.
Heads up to the chefs in here: the saffron is a small lot, about 40 tins. First come. Wholesale pricing for restaurant accounts as usual.
RESTOCK: Aleppo pepper (true Syrian, this year's crop) and wild fennel pollen from Calabria. Fennel pollen is limited — 12 jars. Reply ORDER with quantity.
New: single-origin Ceylon cinnamon quills and green cardamom from the same estate as last year. Both shipping Thursday for orders placed by Wednesday noon.
Chanterelles flushing early this year — beautiful golden batch. Black trumpets maybe two weeks out. Who wants in?
I'll take 5 lbs of the chanterelles for a Friday special. Save me first crack at the black trumpets when they hit, those are non-negotiable.
Early peaches coming in next week, first pick Saturday. Heirloom tomatoes are coming hard right now — if you're running specials you'll want extra.
Building a stone-fruit special around those peaches. Bumping my tomatoes to 3 cases, they're flying. Anyone sourcing good basil this week before I drive up?
Sweetwater here — cutting basil and a gorgeous lemon sorrel tomorrow. I'll set a flat aside for you, Cynthia.
Ironwood: next pork share is ready in two weeks. Berkshire, pasture-raised. Whole and half animals — let me know if you want in for the menu.
Crew — chanterelles and the new tomme land Thursday. Luis, hold the fava work for me. Dani, I want to walk you through the puree before service.
On it chef. I'll break down the fish when it's in. We're at 110 on the books for Friday.
Puree sounds good. I'll have the chèvre tart shells blind-baked before family meal so we can taste the peach plate together.
Nice work last weekend, both Friday and Saturday ran clean. Cynthia, you've got the produce and dairy orders this week — I'm trusting your numbers.
Selling a barely-used 8-burner range and a 2-door reach-in, both maintained. DM if you're building out a kitchen. Pickup in Jersey City.
